# Diagnose My Shot — Inverse Data Layer v2 (coach-interview)

**Generated by** `tools/diagnose-harness-v2.mjs` · **engine** `impact-flight.js` (read-only, byte-identical) · **2026-07-10**
**Emits** `diagnose-map-v2.json` (2.07 MB, cap 4 MB) + this doc. v1 (`diagnose-map.json`) is untouched for the live page.

Everything below falls out of `solveFlight()` grid counts. No invented physics, no hand-tuned probabilities. All computation is local node.

---

## 0. What changed from v1 (and what stayed law)

v1 inverted a single 7-iron grid into 180 descriptor→cause buckets with volume priors + representatives. v2 keeps that method verbatim and adds five things, each derived the same honest way:

1. **Per-club grids** — 7-iron kept byte-for-byte; **driver** added with its own realistic delivery ranges and its own percentile-derived height/distance/speed norms.
2. **Conditioning stats on every cluster** (speed histogram + carry p10/p50/p90 + secondary-miss histogram) so answers reweight clusters **without duplicating maps**.
3. **Secondary-miss math** (`alsoProduces`) — the coach's "do you also hit pulls/pushes?" question, computed by sweeping the face.
4. **Info-gain table** — expected bits each candidate question buys → a recommended ask order inside the 6-tap budget.
5. **Owner acceptance scenarios** run against the shipped, trimmed, rounded JSON.

The v1 honesty doctrine still governs: **shape (curve+start-line) structurally pins the face-to-path relationship** (asserted, calm), while **which exact delivery produced it stays a ranked list with real prior shares** (never a fake 95%). Conditioning narrows that list; it never invents certainty.

### The one engine caveat, stated plainly

`impact-flight.js` has **no driver preset** — its `CLUBS` table is 7-iron only, so `club:'driver'` **falls back to the 7-iron preset** (verified: `solveFlight(…,'driver')` is byte-identical to `solveFlight(…,'7iron')` for equal inputs). The driver grid is therefore the *same shared physics driven through driver-realistic input ranges*. Two honest consequences, both respected by the data layer:

- **(good, real) start-line is correctly more face-dominant at low loft.** The engine's built-in `faceW` loft-taper gives **0.835** at driver loft 13 vs **0.760** at 7-iron loft 28 — a driver starts the ball more where the face points. This is a genuine, physically-motivated difference, not a fudge.
- **(watch it) absolute driver carry compresses at the top.** The shared ball-speed→carry curve is 7-iron-drag-calibrated and saturates early (driver cs 115 → ~225 yd carry, cs 120 → ~227 yd — plausible *shape*, understated *magnitude* at tour speed). So **absolute driver yardage is never treated as truth.** Every driver band is percentile-anchored to the **driver grid's own distribution**, and all conditioning is **relative** (member-count reweighting).

---

## 1. Per-club grids + percentile-derived bucket norms

Height and distance bands are **derived per club** from that club's own grid percentiles (the v1 method, now programmatic). A "High" driver flight is **not** the same ratio as a "High" 7-iron flight, and both are relative to their own well-struck baseline.

| | 7-iron | Driver |
|---|---|---|
| Grid combos | 314 721 | 413 270 |
| Club speed (mph) | 60–110 /5 | 75–120 /5 |
| Face / path (°) | ±8 / ±8 | ±8 / ±8 |
| Attack (°) | -6..4 | -4..6 |
| Dynamic loft (°) | 18..34 /2 | 8..20 /1 |
| Well-struck baseline | attack -3°, loft 28° | attack 2°, loft 13° |
| Height ratio span | 0.7–1.2 | 0.7–1.3 |
| **Height edges** (Low/Norm/High/Balloon @ p20/60/88) | 0.9 / 1 / 1.1 | 0.9 / 1.1 / 1.2 |
| Dist-frac span | -0.088 .. 0.0489 | -0.0036 .. 0.0494 |
| **Distance edges** (Long/Full/Slight/Notice @ p33/75/90) | -0.0362 / 0.0077 / 0.0233 | 0 / 0.0144 / 0.0247 |
| **Speed edges** (slow/mid/fast @ p33/66) | <75 / <95 | <90 / <105 |

**Driver ranges — why these.** Club speed 75–120 mph covers a lady/senior amateur up to a near-tour swing (TrackMan: avg amateur ~93, LPGA ~94, PGA ~113). Attack −4..+6 spans the classic amateur *hitting-down-on-driver* fault through the optimised *hitting up*. Dynamic loft 8–20 spans de-lofted to added-loft/ballooning. The engine's *model* driver carry (7-iron preset fallback) runs cs 75 → ~149 yd, cs 95 → ~196 yd, cs 115 → ~225 yd — believable *shape*, but understated *magnitude* vs real driver yardage (see the honesty correction below). It is used only to *rank/band* deliveries, never quoted as a real carry.

**Speed-band human anchors (curious-golfer copy):**
- **7-iron** — slow: "Gentle 7-iron speed — most of these carry around 106 m. Common for juniors, seniors and newer players (under 75 mph)."  ·  mid: "The middle of the club-golfer range — around 145 m carry. Where most amateurs sit (75–95 mph)."  ·  fast: "Strong / low-handicap speed — around 185 m carry or more (95 mph and up)."
- **Driver** — slow: "Gentle Driver speed — a senior, junior, lady or newer player's swing (under 90 mph)."  ·  mid: "The middle of the amateur driver range (90–105 mph) — where most club golfers sit."  ·  fast: "Strong / low-handicap driver speed (105 mph and up)."
  - *Honesty note (Driver):* StrikeArc models the driver on the engine's shared 7-iron physics (no dedicated driver preset yet), so it reads the shape and cause of your miss — not an exact driver carry. The model's driver carry runs short of real-world yardage at these speeds, so no absolute carry is quoted here; the diagnosis and every band are relative to the driver grid's own distribution and stay correct.

> **Honesty correction (v2.1) — the driver speed anchor no longer quotes a model carry.** The driver grid's own median carries are slow/mid/fast = **147/179/202 m** (161/196/221 yd). These are *genuine* grid medians — but they come from the 7-iron-calibrated ball-speed→carry curve running on the driver's 7-iron-preset fallback (smash capped at ~1.42 vs a real driver's ~1.48–1.50; 7-iron drag). Real-world TrackMan norms put a 90–105 mph amateur at ~205–235 yd carry and a <90 mph swing at ~185–210 yd, so the model *understates* driver carry by ~20–45 yd. The earlier v2 anchor stated "around 179 m carry … where most amateurs sit (90–105 mph)" as curious-golfer fact — a concrete user-facing inaccuracy that contradicted this doc's own rule that **absolute driver yardage is never truth**. Fix (root, honest): the driver anchor now states only the club-speed band (the real, trustworthy *input*) plus a qualitative descriptor, and carries an explicit note that the model reads the *shape/cause* of the miss, not a driver carry number. **No fabricated carry replaces it** — the shipped driver copy contains zero engine-derived yardage. The 7-iron anchor is unchanged (the 7-iron carry curve *is* the engine's calibration target, so "around 145 m carry" is honest). Scope: this only ever touched anchor prose — height/distance banding and all conditioning are relative (member-count reweighting) and were always correct.

### Engine-vs-intuition finding #1 — you cannot lose real distance from delivery geometry alone

Matched-speed, the whole 7-iron grid moves carry only **-8.8%..+4.9%**; the driver only **-0.4%..+4.9%**. The driver baseline (attack +2°, loft 13°) already sits near the low-spin-loft optimum, so **almost every driver delivery only *loses*** — the worst spin-loft excess costs ~4.9% carry. Coaching consequence (and the app must say it): a driver that comes up **way** short is club-speed or strike, **not** delivery geometry. "Way short" therefore promotes the contact card, exactly as the spec's §3.4 already argues.

**Label-vs-magnitude note (issue #2) — driver "Noticeable loss" ⇄ the S3 word "Way short".** Because a driver loses almost nothing from geometry, its distance bands are percentile-anchored to a *tiny* loss range: the driver "Noticeable loss" band is only **2.5%..4.9%** of carry (≈5–10 yd on a ~196 yd drive; 7-iron's is 2.3%..4.9%, similarly small). The S3 UI word for that band is **"Way short"** — which honestly names the *perceived* miss, not the geometry loss the engine can see. The data does **not** overstate the loss (it caps it at the grid's real 4.9% max), and this mismatch is the *teaching point*, not a bug: a driver that is genuinely way short is club-speed or strike, which is exactly why "Way short" promotes the contact card (spec §3.4). `meta.bands.distance` now ships `bandLossPct` + `lossNote` per club so nothing downstream can imply a bigger geometry loss than the grid supports.

---

## 2. Conditioning without map duplication

Instead of a map per answer, each cluster carries the stats needed to reweight it in place:

```js
cluster = {
  cause:{face,path,attack}, priorPct, gridCount,
  representative:{faceAngle,clubPath,attackAngle,dynamicLoft,clubSpeed, spinLoft},  // spinLoft = dynamicLoft − attackAngle (engine line 125)
  ranges:{…},
  speedHist:{slow,mid,fast},          // % of this cluster's members in each per-club speed band
  carryM:{p10,p50,p90},               // metres — cluster's carry distribution (piecewise-linear CDF for ±band answers)
  alsoProduces:{Pull,'Pull-Draw',…},  // 9-flight histogram from the face-timing sweep (§3)
  startLineMix:{Left,Straight,Right},  // coarse Pull/Straight/Push mass
}
```

**The generalised divot-filter pattern (verify the math).** Every answer is Bayes on the cluster identity:

> `posterior(cluster) ∝ prior(cluster) × P(answer | cluster)`, then renormalize the surviving clusters to 100.

- **speed answer** → `P(answer|cluster) = speedHist[answer]/100` (member fraction in that band).
- **absolute carry ±10 m** → `P = CDF(v+10) − CDF(v−10)` from `carryM{p10,p50,p90}`.
- **secondary-miss** → `P(answer|cluster) = startLineMix[family]/100` (Laplace-floored).
- **divot** → hard filter on attack band (the v1 `divotFilter`), then renormalize; empty ⇒ keep all and say so.

Because `prior(cluster) = count/symptomTotal` and `P(answer|cluster) = countInBand/count`, the product renormalizes to `countInBand / Σ countInBand` — **identical to physically re-filtering the grid to the answered band and re-counting.** That equivalence is the proof the stored stats need no map duplication.

### Three worked renormalization proofs (from the shipped JSON)

**Proof 1 — 7-iron · `Slice||Straight||Normal||Slight loss` · speed=fast**  (weight: P(speed=fast|cluster) = speedHist.fast/100)

| cluster (face/path/attack) | prior% | speedHist.fast | posterior% |
|---|---|---|---|
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 72 | 34.3% | **72.6** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 21 | 33.3% | **20.6** |
| slightly open / slightly out-to-in / steep descending | 7 | 33.3% | **6.9** |

Posterior sums to **100.1%** (renormalized). Cross-check vs re-counting the actual grid members in the `fast` band: 20.6% / 72.5% / 6.9% — agreeing with the reweighted posterior to within **±0.1**. The exact identity is `countInBand / Σ countInBand` on the *raw integer counts*; the stored histogram and priors are rounded to 0.1, so the reweight-from-rounded-stats and the exact recount can differ by a single rounding step (here 0.1). That ±0.1 is a rounding artifact, not a modelling error — the stored stats reproduce the member-count posterior to display precision.

**Proof 2 — 7-iron · `Slice||Straight||Normal||Slight loss` · divot=deep**  (weight: keep attack ∈ divotFilter(deep) then renormalize)

| cluster (face/path/attack) | prior% | kept? | posterior% |
|---|---|---|---|
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 72 | yes | **72** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 21 | yes | **21** |
| slightly open / slightly out-to-in / steep descending | 7 | yes | **7** |

Posterior sums to **100.0%** (renormalized). Divot=deep kept only the steep-descending clusters and renormalized — the generalised filter.

**Proof 3 — Driver · `Slice||Straight||Normal||Full (normal distance)` · secondary=pulls**  (weight: P(pulls|cluster) = startLineMix.Left/100 (Laplace-floored))

| cluster (face/path/attack) | prior% | startLineMix.Left | posterior% |
|---|---|---|---|
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / level | 32.5 | 31.5% | **28.1** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / level | 25.3 | 42.9% | **29.8** |
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / moderate descending | 13 | 31.5% | **11.2** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / moderate descending | 10.2 | 42.9% | **12** |
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / ascending | 7.4 | 31.5% | **6.4** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / ascending | 5.5 | 42.9% | **6.5** |
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 3.5 | 31.5% | **3** |
| square / strongly out-to-in / steep descending | 2.6 | 42.9% | **3.1** |

Posterior sums to **100.1%** (renormalized). 

---

## 3. Secondary-miss pattern math (the coach's "do you also hit…?")

**Model (standard coaching causality):** a player's recurring pattern is *one delivery with the face varying shot-to-shot* while path/attack/loft/speed stay near-constant. Computed honestly from the engine: for each cluster we hold path/attack/loft/speed at the representative and **sweep the face across ±6° (1° steps, clamped to the grid's ±8°)**, classifying each shot into the classic 9-ball-flight window (3 start lines × 3 curve directions). Laplace-smoothed (α=0.5) so nothing hits a hard zero.

`alsoProduces`: Pull · Pull-Draw · Pull-Fade · Straight · Draw · Fade · Push · Push-Draw · Push-Fade. `startLineMix` = the coarse Pull(Left)/Straight/Push(Right) mass. **Evidence rule:** "I also hit pulls often" multiplies each cluster's weight by `P(Pull|cluster) = startLineMix.Left/100`, then renormalize. (A pull = the ball *starts left*; the D-plane truth is that an out-to-in path shifts the whole start-line distribution left, so out-to-in deliveries produce more pulls — this is what makes the answer discriminate.)

### Flagship validation — "also pulls" must strengthen out-to-in over open-face-neutral

**Step 1 — the clean mechanism (controlled path-isolation probe).** Hold face/attack/loft/speed fixed and sweep only the face; count the pull (Left-start) fraction as clubPath moves from down-the-line to out-to-in:

| club, face centre | path 0° | path −3° | path −6° |
|---|---|---|---|
| Driver, face +5° | 0% | 0% | 7.7% |
| Driver, face +3° | 15.4% | 15.4% | 23.1% |
| 7-iron, face +3° | 15.4% | 15.4% | 23.1% |

Pull fraction **rises monotonically as the path turns out-to-in** — with face held constant. So the coaching causality is real in the engine: an out-to-in path shifts the whole start-line distribution left, so those deliveries genuinely also produce pulls. "Also pulls" is a legitimate discriminator.

**Step 2 — end-to-end on the shipped bucket.** Driver everyday slice `Slice||Right||Normal||Full (normal distance)` (starts right — the one slice shape that leaves the path AMBIGUOUS; a *straight*-starting slice is structurally forced to out-to-in and has nothing to disambiguate). Story path mass, before vs after "also pulls" — both shares are among the **≥1% patterns** (the conditioning model renormalizes survivors on each answer), so the shift isolates the reweighting effect from the <1% tail being set aside:

| story (face / path) | before% (≥1% patterns) | after "also pulls"% |
|---|---|---|
| strongly open / strongly out-to-in | 38.7 | 32.9 |
| strongly open / slightly out-to-in | 29.1 | 24.7 |
| strongly open / neutral | 13.7 | 12.5 |
| slightly open / strongly out-to-in | 12.6 | 20.5 |
| slightly open / slightly out-to-in | 5.8 | 9.4 |

Out-to-in path mass 86.2% → **87.5%** (↑); neutral-path mass 13.7% → 12.5% (↓). **CONFIRMED.** (The *standalone* baseline the UI shows first is the true all-delivery share — top story 37.6% + a 2.8% <1% tail, §5(c); here we use the renormalized survivor basis because that is the basis the answer reweights.)

**Honest nuance (reported, not hidden):** the effect is modest because the engine's dominant lever is *how far the face must travel to reach a left start*, which couples face-openness and path. A wildly-open face (+6°) barely reaches a pull inside a ±6° window regardless of path; a slightly-open face (+2–3°) pulls readily, and out-to-in path adds to that. So "also pulls" most strongly upweights the *slightly-open, out-to-in* stories — which is exactly the coach's over-the-top slicer. We do not overclaim a pure path mechanism; the controlled probe above is the clean isolated proof, the bucket table is the realistic aggregate.

### Engine-vs-intuition finding #2 — a true pull-HOOK is the snap-hook (in-to-out + shut face), never over-the-top

Scenario (f) failed the *naive* expectation ("pull-hook = closed face + out-to-in path") — and the engine is right to. A Hook needs a **>6° closed face-to-path gap**. If the path is already out-to-in (leftward), opening a 6°-closed gap needs the face **beyond −8°**, off the realistic grid. Proof — a shut face swept across paths (7-iron, attack −3°, loft 24°):

| clubPath | face −8° | face −7° | face −6° |
|---|---|---|---|
| -6° (strongly out-to-in) | Pull Draw | Pull Draw | Pull |
| -3° (slightly out-to-in) | Pull Draw | Pull Draw | Pull Draw |
| -1° (neutral) | Pull Hook | Pull Draw | Pull Draw |
| +2° (slightly in-to-out) | Pull Hook | Pull Hook | Pull Hook |
| +6° (strongly in-to-out) | Pull Hook | Pull Hook | Pull Hook |

Out-to-in paths (−6°, −3°) can only reach a **Pull-Draw**; the **Pull-Hook appears only from neutral/in-to-out paths** — the snap-hook / "double-cross" that flies low and runs. Every `Hook||Left` bucket in the map reflects this: the top stories are *strongly closed face × (strongly/slightly in-to-out or neutral) path*. This is a genuine ball-flight truth (not a bug), and the coach-interview should teach it: "your low duck-hook is an in-to-out path with a slammed-shut face, not an over-the-top move." The data layer stores the honest result; the naive expectation is what's wrong.

---

## 4. Information-gain table (which question to ask, in what order)

Expected mutual information `I(delivery archetype ; answer)` in **bits**, over each shape-conditioned population (what we know after the 9-flight picker + severity, *before* height/distance are asked). Archetype = face|path|attack|loft-band|speed-band; face|path is already ~pinned by shape, so gains here are about resolving **attack, loft and speed**. `attackKnown` is the ceiling (a perfect divot).

| shape posterior (7-iron) | H₀ | speed | height | rel-dist | abs-carry ±10 | secondary (arch → story*) | divot | (attack ceiling) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Everyday slice — starts right, curves right (path AMBIGUOUS) | 7.028 | 1.573 | 1.125 | 1.17 | 1.384 | 0.03 → **0.024** | 1.292 | 1.981 |
| Slice — starts straight, curves right (path PINNED out-to-in) | 6.164 | 1.573 | 1.151 | 1.204 | 1.368 | 0.003 → **0.009** | 1.292 | 1.981 |
| Pull-hook — starts left, curves left | 7.028 | 1.573 | 1.125 | 1.17 | 1.384 | 0.03 → **0.024** | 1.292 | 1.981 |
| Push — starts right, flies straight | 6.069 | 1.573 | 1.133 | 1.18 | 1.379 | 0.068 → **0.061** | 1.292 | 1.981 |

\* *secondary (arch → story)*: the left number is bits toward the full delivery archetype (small — path is a minor share of archetype entropy); the **bold** number is bits toward the **path/story alone** (`I(face|path ; also-pulls)`), which is what the question actually resolves. Story entropy for the start-right slice is 1.959 bits; "also pulls" removes 0.024 of them.

**Reading it.** Start line is collected *free* by the 9-flight picker and is the single biggest disambiguator (symptom-level info gain **+1.081 bits**, 7-iron) — already spent before any of the above.

Two honest structural notes the table makes visible:

- **speed = 1.573 bits is constant across every shape** — it exactly equals the entropy of the speed-band marginal, because club speed is *independent of the face/path/attack fault*. Those bits are real but **orthogonal**: they pin the delivery's *speed/yardage number*, not the *cause*. Same story, weaker, for absolute carry (1.384).
- **secondary-miss is a WEAK disambiguator — nearly redundant with start line.** Even measured against its true target (the path/story), it removes only **0.024 of 1.959 story-bits** on the start-right slice, and **0.009** on the start-*straight* slice (which is already pinned to out-to-in). Against the full archetype: 0.03 / 0.003. **Honest reason:** "also pulls?" and the start-line column of the picker probe the *same start-direction axis* — start line already captured most of what the secondary pattern would tell you, so the explicit question adds little. Its value is *pedagogical and directional* (it's the coach-natural question, and it moves the ranking the right way — flagship §3), **not** information-theoretic. Do not treat it as load-bearing.

Ranked by raw archetype bits on the everyday (start-right) slice (⚠ speed & carry top the list but are *orthogonal magnitude-probes* per the note above, not fault-resolvers): **speed band** (1.573) > **absolute carry ±10 m** (1.384) > **divot** (1.292) > **relative distance** (1.17) > **height** (1.125) > **secondary-miss** (0.03). Divot's ceiling (perfect attack) is **1.981 bits** — attack is the residual fog once shape is known (v1's finding, reconfirmed per club).

**Recommended ask order (≤6 taps pre-reveal):**

1. **Shape** — 9-flight picker (start line + curve). *Free, biggest split.* (1–2 taps incl. severity.)
2. **Height** — keys the map; leaks loft & attack via spin-loft (1.125 bits).
3. **Relative distance** — keys the map; also the "way-short ⇒ contact card" trigger (1.17 bits).
4. **Divot** — highest-value refiner of the residual attack fog (realises 1.292 of the 1.981-bit ceiling). Earns the last pre-reveal slot.

**Post-reveal refiners** (on the results card, not pre-reveal):
- **secondary-miss** — a *confirmation*, not a disambiguator (only 0.024 story-bits; largely redundant with start line). Offer it as the coach-natural "does it also…?" beat that builds trust and nudges the ranking directionally; skip it on start-straight shapes where it's ~0.
- **speed band / absolute carry** — offer when the user wants the exact "try this" numbers; they resolve the delivery's magnitude, not the fault. Club is the top-level selector (given), not a within-posterior question.

---

## 5. Owner acceptance scenarios (all run against the shipped JSON)

Top-2 stories are `face / path pct%`. The **baseline** line is the *true all-delivery share* (stored `priorPct`, no renorm) with the <1% tail carried as `+X% rarer patterns` — exactly the documented `consolidateStories(entry)` API (§6). When an answer is applied, the Bayes model renormalizes the ≥1% survivors to 100 (the <1% tail can't be reweighted, so it is set aside); those `+ cond=…` lines are therefore on the **survivor basis**, shown after a survivor-basis baseline so the before→after is like-for-like. "Coach story" is generated from the top story's stored numbers (the dynamic-loft → spin-loft chain is `spinLoft = dynamicLoft − attackAngle`, straight from the engine).

### (a) 7-iron · mid speed · slice · HIGH · noticeably short (the coach's own diagnosis)

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → slightly open / strongly out-to-in 71%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 19.3% _(all-delivery shares; +0.7% rarer <1% patterns)_
- `baseline · ≥1% patterns (conditioning basis)` → slightly open / strongly out-to-in 71.7%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 19.5% _(renormalized survivors — the basis each answer reweights)_
- `+ speed=mid` → slightly open / strongly out-to-in 71.8%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 19.5% _(survivor basis)_
- **PASS** — top loft 33° (added-loft), spinLoft 38°, face-to-path +9.5° (open to path)
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+2.9° vs -6.6°) is the whole slice; 33° delivered loft minus a -5° attack = 38° spin loft (a lot of spin loft — it climbs and bleeds speed).*

### (b) 7-iron · mid speed · slice · LOW · noticeably short (contrast to a)

- *Exact bucket absent; used nearest (height → `Slice||Straight||Normal||Noticeable loss`).*
- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → slightly open / strongly out-to-in 73.3%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 20%
- **PASS** — handled below (S-MISS expected — see finding)
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+2.9° vs -6.6°) is the whole slice; 30° delivered loft minus a -6° attack = 36° spin loft (a lot of spin loft — it climbs and bleeds speed).*

### (b2) 7-iron · slice · LOW · (nearest real low slice) — loft story must FLIP vs a

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → slightly open / strongly out-to-in 68.6%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 31.4%
- **PASS** — low-flight slice top loft 21.5° (de-lofted) vs scenario-a's added-loft ≥30° — materially different loft story
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+2.6° vs -6.6°) is the whole slice; 21.5° delivered loft minus a -5.4° attack = 26.9° spin loft (healthy spin loft).*

### (c) Driver · slice (starts right) · "also pulls often" → out-to-in path stories strengthen

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 37.6%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 28.3% _(all-delivery shares; +2.8% rarer <1% patterns)_
- `baseline · ≥1% patterns (conditioning basis)` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 38.7%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 29.1% _(renormalized survivors — the basis each answer reweights)_
- `+ secondary=pulls` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 32.9%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 24.7% _(survivor basis)_
- **PASS** — out-to-in path mass 86.2% → 87.5% (↑); neutral-path mass 13.7% → 12.5% (↓) after "also pulls" — shares among the ≥1% patterns (the model renormalizes survivors on each answer)
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+6.5° vs -6.5°) is the whole slice; 13.1° delivered loft minus a -0.1° attack = 13.2° spin loft (healthy driver spin loft).*

### (d) Driver · fast · ballooned · way short → spin-loft excess

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → square / slightly out-to-in 44.5%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 16.7%
- `baseline · ≥1% patterns (conditioning basis)` → square / slightly out-to-in 44.4%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 16.7% _(renormalized survivors — the basis each answer reweights)_
- `+ speed=fast` → square / slightly out-to-in 44.4%  ·  square / strongly out-to-in 16.7% _(survivor basis)_
- **PASS** — top story spinLoft 20° (loft 20° − attack 0°) — driver spin-loft excess (grid max ≈ 24°)
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+0.1° vs -2.9°) is the whole fade; 20° delivered loft minus a +0° attack = 20° spin loft (a lot of spin loft for a driver — it balloons and bleeds carry).*

### (e) 7-iron · push that sometimes becomes push-draw → in-to-out path, face timing

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → strongly open / strongly in-to-out 57.1%  ·  slightly open / slightly in-to-out 42.8%
- `baseline · ≥1% patterns (conditioning basis)` → strongly open / strongly in-to-out 57.1%  ·  slightly open / slightly in-to-out 42.8% _(renormalized survivors — the basis each answer reweights)_
- `+ secondary=pushes` → strongly open / strongly in-to-out 66.9%  ·  slightly open / slightly in-to-out 33.2% _(survivor basis)_
- **PASS** — top story path "strongly in-to-out" (in-to-out), rep path +6.5°, face +6.5° matched-right; this cluster's face-timing sweep produces Push-Draw 48.1% of the time (draws confirm the in-to-out path)
- Coach story: *Face matched to path (+6.5° vs +6.5°) is the whole straight; 25.3° delivered loft minus a -4.8° attack = 30.1° spin loft (healthy spin loft).*

### (f) 7-iron · pull-hook · low & running → shut face + in-to-out path (the snap-hook — see finding #2)

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → strongly closed / strongly in-to-out 39%  ·  strongly closed / slightly in-to-out 29.3%
- **PASS** — top story face -6.5° (shut) · path 6.5° (strongly in-to-out) — the snap-hook. Engine disagrees with the "out-to-in pull-hook" intuition: a shut face on an out-to-in path can only make a pull-DRAW (finding #2)
- Coach story: *Face shut to path (-6.5° vs +6.5°) is the whole hook; 18.9° delivered loft minus a -4.9° attack = 23.8° spin loft (low spin loft — it comes out hot and flat).*

### (g) 7-iron · the pure shot (Straight/Straight/Normal/Full) — the wink case

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → square / neutral 100.1%
- **PASS** — top story face 0° · path 0° — square & down the line
- Coach story: *Face matched to path (+0° vs +0°) is the whole straight; 25.3° delivered loft minus a -4.8° attack = 30.1° spin loft (healthy spin loft).*

### (h) 7-iron · everyday over-the-top slice + divot=deep refiner

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 42.4%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 31.8%
- `baseline · ≥1% patterns (conditioning basis)` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 42.4%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 31.8% _(renormalized survivors — the basis each answer reweights)_
- `+ divot=deep` → strongly open / strongly out-to-in 42.4%  ·  strongly open / slightly out-to-in 31.8% _(survivor basis)_
- **PASS** — before: strongly open/strongly out-to-in 42.4%; after divot=deep top variant attack="steep descending"
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+6.5° vs -6.5°) is the whole slice; 28.6° delivered loft minus a -5.8° attack = 34.4° spin loft (a lot of spin loft — it climbs and bleeds speed).*

### (i) 7-iron · weak high fade that loses a little → open face + added loft

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → strongly open / slightly in-to-out 33.3%  ·  slightly open / neutral 19.5%
- **PASS** — face-to-path +3.5° (open), loft 30.9° (added), spinLoft 33.3°
- Coach story: *Face open to path (+6.5° vs +3°) is the whole fade; 30.9° delivered loft minus a -2.4° attack = 33.3° spin loft (healthy spin loft).*

### (j) 7-iron · low draw that runs out (gains distance) → de-lofted, face shut to path

- `baseline (shape+height+distance)` → square / slightly in-to-out 44.4%  ·  slightly closed / slightly in-to-out 16.7% _(all-delivery shares; +0.9% rarer <1% patterns)_
- **PASS** — face-to-path -3.0° (shut to path → draw), loft 18.9° (de-lofted), spinLoft 23.8°
- Coach story: *Face shut to path (-0.1° vs +2.9°) is the whole draw; 18.9° delivered loft minus a -4.9° attack = 23.8° spin loft (low spin loft — it comes out hot and flat).*

**Scenario tally: 11/11 pass.**

### Engine-vs-intuition finding #3 — a LOW slice that's also *noticeably short* does not exist

Scenario (b) is an S-MISS on purpose. Low flight comes from **de-lofting** (hands forward, less delivered loft) — which *lowers* spin loft, *raises* smash and if anything *adds* carry. So the grid has no "Low + Noticeable-loss" slice: low-and-short can only be **contact or club speed**, never delivery geometry. The nearest real low slice (scenario b2) has a **de-lofted** loft story — the exact inverse of scenario (a)'s added-loft story — which is precisely the coaching contrast the owner asked to see.

---

## 6. Size + the exact client API for `diagnose-engine-v2.js`

`diagnose-map-v2.json` = **2.07 MB** (cap 4 MB). Clusters below **1% prior** are folded into a per-key `otherPct`/`otherClusterCount`; all values rounded to 1 decimal.

Signatures in the §2.1 style of `docs/diagnose-spec.md` (this run defines the DATA + reference math; the UI file is a later commit):

```js
import { solveFlight, trajectorySamples, shapeLabel } from './impact-flight.js';

export async function loadMapV2(url = './diagnose-map-v2.json')   // fetch + module-scope cache
export function clubMap(map, club)          // map.clubs['7iron'|'driver'] — throws on unknown club
export function descriptorKey({curve,startLine,height,distanceLoss})  // `${curve}||${startLine}||${height}||${distanceLoss}`
export function lookup(clubMap, descriptor)
  // -> { entry, exact:true, key } | { entry:null, nearest:[{descriptor,changed,key}…≤3], key }
  // relaxation order (unchanged from v1): distanceLoss ±1 → height ±1 → curve severity swap
export function consolidateStories(entry)
  // group entry.clusters by face|path; story = { face, path, pct:ΣpriorPct, rep, variants:[{attack,pct,rep}] }
  // returns { stories(sorted desc), otherPct, otherClusterCount, clusterCount, gridCount }

// ── conditioning pipeline (all pure; each renormalizes clusters to 100, then re-consolidate) ──
export function reweightSpeed(clusters, band)     // band ∈ 'slow'|'mid'|'fast'; w = speedHist[band]/100
export function reweightCarry(clusters, metres, band=10) // w = carryCDF(m+band) − carryCDF(m−band) from carryM{p10,p50,p90}
export function reweightSecondary(clusters, ans)  // ans ∈ 'pulls'|'straights'|'pushes'; w = startLineMix[Left|Straight|Right]/100 (floored)
export function divotFilter(answer)               // 'deep'→{'steep descending'} · 'brushed'→{'moderate descending','level'} · 'none'→{'level','ascending'} · 'unsure'→null
export function applyDivot(clusters, answer)      // hard-filter attack band; empty ⇒ {clusters, empty:true} (keep all, say so)
export function reweight(clusters, wOf)           // generic: posterior ∝ prior × wOf(cluster), renormalize

// ── evidence copy: the reference-anchor strings live in map.clubs[club].meta.bands ──
//   speed band anchors  → meta.bands.speed.anchorCopy.{slow,mid,fast}
//     · meta.bands.speed.absoluteCarryTrusted — false ⇒ anchor quotes NO carry (driver);
//       meta.bands.speed.note carries the honest "why" (surface it beside the anchor).
//   height/dist edges   → meta.bands.{height,distance}.edges + labels   (never free-typed)
//     · meta.bands.distance.bandLossPct[i] = [lo%,hi%] carry-loss for label i;
//       meta.bands.distance.lossNote explains the "Way short" ⇄ tiny-geometry-loss framing.

export function needsFollowUp(stories)            // unchanged: false only if stories[0].pct≥60 AND top variant/pct≥0.7
export function solveTrace(rep)                   // solveFlight(rep) → { flight, samples } for the ember reveal
export function spinLoftOf(rep)                   // rep.dynamicLoft − rep.attackAngle (already stored as rep.spinLoft)
```

**Band definitions + reference-anchor copy (shipped inside the map, per club):**

- **7-iron** — height edges (ratio) `0.9 / 1 / 1.1` · distance edges (frac) `-0.0362 / 0.0077 / 0.0233` (worst "Noticeable loss" 2.3%..4.9% carry) · speed edges (mph) `75 / 95` · speed anchors → `meta.bands.speed.anchorCopy` (quotes carry (calibrated club)).
- **Driver** — height edges (ratio) `0.9 / 1.1 / 1.2` · distance edges (frac) `0 / 0.0144 / 0.0247` (worst "Noticeable loss" 2.5%..4.9% carry) · speed edges (mph) `90 / 105` · speed anchors → `meta.bands.speed.anchorCopy` (quotes NO carry — mph band only (see note)).

### Conditioning call order (client)

```
lookup → consolidateStories → render stories
  → (optional, any order, each re-consolidates) reweightSpeed / reweightCarry / reweightSecondary / applyDivot
  → re-render with 'REFINED BY…' eyebrow; ember redraws from new top story's rep
```

All conditioning is commutative-safe only per the Bayes model above; the UI applies at most one or two refiners, so order is a UX choice, not a correctness one. Every percentage on screen is a grid-count share; every ember flight is a real `solveFlight` solve of a stored representative.
